items.summary: Create a backend data model to store and manage people with fields for id (unique identifier), name (Text), and createdAt (Int timestamp for creation date)

Implement backend CRUD functions for people: createPerson(name: Text) returns Person, listPeople() returns all people sorted by name, updatePerson(id, name) updates person name, deletePerson(id) removes person and returns success status
Create a People Management component with a list view showing all people and an add person form with name input field, using the same styling patterns as ExpenseForm and ExpenseList components
Add edit functionality to People Management component allowing inline editing of person names with save and cancel actions
Add delete functionality to People Management component with confirmation dialog before removing a person
Update ExpenseForm component to replace the free-text person name input with a searchable dropdown selector populated from the managed people list, maintaining the current form layout and validation
Add quick-add functionality to ExpenseForm person selector allowing users to create new people directly from the dropdown without leaving the expense entry form

Implement backend query function getPersonExpenses(personId: Text, year: ?Nat, month: ?Nat) that returns all expenses for a specific person, optionally filtered by year and/or month, sorted by date descending

Create a PersonDetailView component that displays a specific person's complete expense history in a table format matching ExpenseList styling, showing all expense details (amount, description, date, category) sorted by date
Add summary statistics section to PersonDetailView showing total spending in ₹ and daily average calculated across all time for that person
Add a People tab to the main App navigation alongside Expenses and Summary tabs, displaying the People Management component when selected
Add clickable links from person names in ExpenseList and PersonBreakdown components that navigate to the PersonDetailView for that specific person

Create React Query hooks in useQueries.ts for people management: useCreatePerson, useListPeople, useUpdatePerson, useDeletePerson, and usePersonExpenses following the existing pattern for expense hooks
